title: Paper Walkthrough — Geometrically-Constrained Agent for Spatial Reasoning
summary: How a formal task constraint bridges the semantic-to-geometric gap in spatial reasoning VLMsCan a vision-language model (VLM) imagine sitting on a sofa and see where the coffee is?Logically, it’s possible — work out the sofa’s orientation, rotate its imagined viewpoint accordingly, and locate the coffee table relative to that viewpoint — but it falls apart in reality.
